policy.marketOverlay: NASDAQ Composite via FRED is used as the long market-index proxy. DJIA is cited for context but not redistributed as a long raw series because of source copyright restrictions.
policy.longView: U.S. gasoline before 1990 is annual EIA historical context. From 1990 forward, the chart uses weekly EIA/FRED observations.
policy.europeOverlay: European Commission Weekly Oil Bulletin EU average Euro-super 95 prices are converted from EUR/liter to USD/gallon using European Central Bank USD per EUR reference rates.
policy.inflation: Inflation-adjusted values use CPIAUCSL from FRED and are expressed in 2026-04 dollars. The Europe overlay is converted to USD/gallon first and then adjusted with U.S. CPI for dollar comparison; it is not a local EU inflation adjustment.
policy.oilStocks: Oil-company stock performance uses Nasdaq historical close chart data, sampled monthly and indexed to 100 at the visible start date. It is price performance only, not dividend-adjusted total return.

oilStockSeries.name: Shell
oilStockSeries.sourceName: Shell PLC American Depositary Shares (each representing two (2) Ordinary Shares)

events.title: Suez Crisis disrupts oil transit
events.summary: The Suez Crisis is widely identified as a postwar oil shock tied to Middle East conflict and transit disruption.
events.whyIncluded: Adds Cold War-era context before modern weekly gasoline data begins.

events.title: OPEC is founded
events.summary: OPEC was created by Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Venezuela to coordinate petroleum policies.
events.whyIncluded: OPEC later became a central institution in global crude oil supply and pricing dynamics.

events.title: Arab oil embargo begins
events.summary: Arab oil producers imposed an embargo and production cuts that sharply increased world oil prices.
events.whyIncluded: One of the defining oil shocks of the Cold War era and a major gasoline-price inflection point.

events.title: Iranian Revolution oil shock
events.summary: Iranian oil output fell sharply during the revolution, contributing to the second major oil shock of the 1970s.
events.whyIncluded: Explains the late-1970s gasoline-price jump visible in annual data.

events.title: Iran-Iraq War begins
events.summary: The Iran-Iraq War followed the Iranian Revolution and is identified in historical oil-shock literature as a major postwar oil-market event.
events.whyIncluded: Adds regional-war context for the early-1980s energy-price environment.

events.title: Iraq invades Kuwait
events.summary: The invasion disrupted Iraqi and Kuwaiti supply and contributed to a rapid crude oil price run-up.
events.whyIncluded: A canonical geopolitical oil shock inside the weekly gasoline price record.

events.title: Oil prices slump during weak demand period
events.summary: Weak petroleum demand and OPEC production choices helped push oil prices sharply lower in the late 1990s.
events.whyIncluded: Shows that gas-price pressure can come from demand weakness, not only wars or supply shocks.

events.title: OPEC production cuts help reverse price collapse
events.summary: A late-1990s oil price shock reversed as OPEC and other producers reduced supply after the 1998 collapse.
events.whyIncluded: A clear example of coordinated producer action affecting oil-market balance.

events.title: Venezuelan oil strike
events.summary: A major Venezuelan strike disrupted oil production and exports, adding pressure to global crude markets.
events.whyIncluded: EIA identifies the strike as one of the major recent crude oil supply disruptions.

events.title: U.S.-led invasion of Iraq
events.summary: Middle East hostilities and supply-risk concerns were part of the early-2000s oil-price environment.
events.whyIncluded: A geopolitically important moment near the beginning of the 2000s oil-price climb.

events.title: Oil price spike before financial crisis collapse
events.summary: Oil prices reached extreme highs in 2008 before the global financial crisis drove demand and prices downward.
events.whyIncluded: A major modern example of gasoline prices, crude oil, and macro stress moving together.

events.title: Libya conflict and Arab Spring supply risk
events.summary: Political instability in oil-producing regions raised supply-risk concerns during a period of elevated crude prices.
events.whyIncluded: A post-2008 geopolitical risk period visible in oil and gasoline markets.

events.title: OPEC maintains output amid oil-price collapse
events.summary: Global crude markets shifted into oversupply, contributing to a steep oil-price decline from 2014 into 2016.
events.whyIncluded: A major OPEC-era supply decision that coincides with a clear gasoline-price downshift.

events.title: Attack on Saudi Aramco Abqaiq and Khurais facilities
events.summary: Attacks disrupted Saudi crude production and showed how disruption risk can move prices rapidly.
events.whyIncluded: EIA specifically analyzes the immediate price effect of this supply-disruption risk.

events.title: COVID-19 emergency and gasoline demand collapse
events.summary: Pandemic responses sharply reduced travel and gasoline demand, pushing U.S. gasoline prices to multi-year lows.
events.whyIncluded: A demand-side shock that strongly affected both fuel prices and equity markets.

events.title: Russia further invades Ukraine
events.summary: Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine and sanctions risk pushed crude oil above $100 per barrel and raised gasoline-price expectations.
events.whyIncluded: One of the clearest recent geopolitical shocks in both gasoline and market data.

events.title: Crude prices peak then ease as supply and demand conditions shift
events.summary: Crude prices generally fell after early June 2022 as recession concerns, China demand weakness, and strategic reserve releases affected supply-demand balance.
events.whyIncluded: Shows that policy, demand, and inventories can matter alongside war-related risk.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz chokepoint context
events.summary: EIA identifies the Strait of Hormuz as the world's most important oil transit chokepoint, with roughly one-fifth of global petroleum liquids consumption moving through it in recent years.
events.whyIncluded: Adds trusted context for interpreting Persian Gulf blockade or shipping-risk scenarios without claiming an unsourced closure event.
